name: Dartmouth Crab and Lobster Salad
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Cooked, shelled, sliced lobster tail
1 pinch Salt and pepper to taste
1 pinch Salt and pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, combine lump crabmeat and lobster. Season with lemon juice, salt, and pepper; gently toss to mix. Cover and refrigerate.
2. For the Fennel and Orange Salad:
- Using a vegetable peeler, remove 1/4 of the zest from 1 orange. Cut into very fine julienne strips. Blanch in boiling water for 5 minutes, then drain.
- Peel oranges and section them; squeeze out any remaining juice. Trim fennel, reserving tops. Remove tough outer layers from fennel bulb and slice thinly.
- Add the fennel slices to the orange mixture. Chop the fennel tops and add to the salad. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Cover and refrigerate.
3. For the Courchamps Sauce:
- In a blender, combine flaked crab, lemon juice, anisette (or dry white wine), chopped parsley and tarragon, soy sauce, and mustard. Blend until pureed.
- With the blender running, gradually add olive oil in a thin stream until blended. Stir in salt and pepper to taste.
4. To serve: Divide mounds of crab salad and fennel salad onto 4 serving plates. Garnish with sauce, chopped Italian parsley, fennel sprigs, and lemon wedges.
